_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 4-6 Pediatric Fever (Revised 08/11/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Obstetrics and Pediatrics Protocol Section 4-6: Pediatric Fever — MDHHS Approved 8/11/23, Reviewed 2023
purpose: Assist EMS providers in reducing fever in pediatric patients prior to arrival to the emergency department. Fever is defined as temperature ≥ 100.4°F (38°C). Emergency management includes assessment for associated problems requiring emergency treatment.

sections.title: Pediatric Fever Treatment
points: Pediatric patients (< 14 years) utilize MI-MEDIC cards for appropriate medication dosage — when unavailable, utilize pediatric dosing listed within protocol
Obtain baseline temperature and document method used
Children < 60 days old: document rectal temperature before administering any antipyretic
Administer antipyretic according to MCA selection; MCA must select at least one: ibuprofen for children > 6 months of age or acetaminophen
Administer ibuprofen only if the child is over 6 months old, has not received ibuprofen or medication containing ibuprofen in the last 6 hours, and is alert
Administer acetaminophen only if the child has not received acetaminophen or medication containing acetaminophen in the last 4 hours and is alert
If the patient's weight is known, use that weight and MI-MEDIC for dosing; if weight is unavailable, use length-based tape and MI-MEDIC; if MI-MEDIC is unavailable, use the dosing table
If there is any question concerning alertness or ability to swallow, DO NOT ADMINISTER
Dosing questions should be directed to online Medical Control

sections.title: Children's Elixir Dosing Table — Acetaminophen 160 mg/5 mL
Children's Elixir Dosing Table — Ibuprofen 100 mg/5 mL
points: Grey (0–2 mo, 3–5 kg): DO NOT GIVE
Pink (3–6 mo, 6–7 kg): DO NOT GIVE

mca_selections_required.item: Antipyretic Selection — Must Select at Least One
mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select at least one antipyretic option for pediatric fever. Both may be selected. Ibuprofen is contraindicated in children ≤ 6 months.
options: Ibuprofen (children > 6 months) — 10 mg/kg PO per Children's Elixir Dosing Table (100 mg/5 mL)
Acetaminophen — 15 mg/kg PO per Children's Elixir Dosing Table (160 mg/5 mL)

key_drugs.name: Acetaminophen PO (Pediatric Fever)
key_drugs.dose: Per Children's Elixir Dosing Table (160 mg/5 mL): Grey 40 mg, Pink 96 mg, Red 128 mg, Purple 160 mg, Yellow 192 mg, White 224 mg, Blue 288 mg, Orange 384 mg, Green 480 mg
key_drugs.contraindications: Children < 60 days — document rectal temperature before administration; do not give if dose received within last 4 hours; known hepatic failure
key_drugs.notes: Use MI-MEDIC when available. Document rectal temperature for children < 60 days before administering.

key_drugs.name: Ibuprofen PO (Pediatric Fever)
key_drugs.dose: Per Children's Elixir Dosing Table (100 mg/5 mL): Red 80 mg, Purple 100 mg, Yellow 120 mg, White 150 mg, Blue 190 mg, Orange 260 mg, Green 300 mg; do not give to Grey or Pink zones
key_drugs.contraindications: Children ≤ 6 months — DO NOT ADMINISTER; do not give if dose received within last 4 hours; known renal failure; dehydration
key_drugs.scope: Per MCA selection — children > 6 months only
key_drugs.notes: Contraindicated in children ≤ 6 months of age. Do not use in pregnant patients.

deterioration_flags: Child < 60 days with fever — obtain rectal temperature before antipyretic; transport promptly
Febrile seizure — refer to Pediatric Seizures-Treatment Protocol
Question concerning alertness or ability to swallow — do not administer oral antipyretic; contact Medical Control as needed
scope_notes: Temperature assessment, passive cooling, oral antipyretic administration: all levels
MCA must select at least one antipyretic (ibuprofen or acetaminophen or both)
Ibuprofen contraindicated in children ≤ 6 months — acetaminophen only for this age group
Children < 60 days: document rectal temperature before antipyretic
Use known weight with MI-MEDIC when available; otherwise use length-based tape and MI-MEDIC; use table only when MI-MEDIC unavailable
